message:
docs(project): update bv workflow references and beads metadata

Update bv (beads viewer) command references across project
documentation to reflect the current CLI surface, replacing
deprecated `--robot-triage` with the recommended `--robot-plan` +
`--robot-priority` + `br ready --json` workflow.

AGENTS.md:
- Rewrite bv workflow section with updated order of operations:
  --robot-plan for primary triage, --robot-priority for sanity
  checks, --robot-insights for deep graph metrics, br ready --json
  for ground-truth actionable issues
- Add tombstone guard documentation warning against claiming
  status=tombstone items and requiring `br show <id>` verification
- Add --robot-recipes command to planning table
- Streamline graph analysis command reference

PLAN_TO_COMPLETE_PORT.md:
- Replace `bv --robot-triage` references with `bv --robot-plan`
  and `bv --robot-priority` in the authoritative plan section and
  current priorities section

docs/program-governance.md:
- Update bead backlog grooming references to use --robot-plan +
  br ready --json
- Update rollup owner checkpoint procedure to include tombstone
  validation via br show
- Update roadmap review and conformance regression triage procedures

.beads/issues.jsonl:
- Add new beads tracking entries from concurrent agent work

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-17o","title":"RPC tests: replace MockProvider with VCR playback","description":"Goal:
- Remove MockProvider usage in tests/rpc_mode.rs and use VCR playback (real recorded streams) to validate RPC streaming behavior without mocks.

Scope:
- Replace MockProvider with a VCR-backed provider or adapter that replays recorded SSE chunks.
- Add cassette naming conventions for RPC scenarios (e.g., rpc_basic.json, rpc_toolcall.json).
- Validate real event sequences, stop reasons, and error propagation.
- Force VCR playback mode in tests (no network) and assert zero live HTTP calls.
- Emit detailed logs on each test: cassette path, expected events, actual events, and diffs.

Logging Requirements:
- Use TestLogger (bd-3ml) for per-test logs and auto-dump on failure.
- Log cassette path, parsed event timeline, and mismatch details (first divergent event + context).

Acceptance Criteria:
- tests/rpc_mode.rs contains no MockProvider/fake stream.
- All RPC tests run in playback mode with recorded cassettes.
- Logs include cassette name/path, parsed event timeline, and assertion diffs on failure.
- CI runs without network and still validates streaming edge cases.

Dependencies:
- Requires VCR infrastructure (bd-1pf).
- Requires recorded cassette(s) (bd-30u or RPC-specific capture).","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss

+{"id":"bd-18fcw","title":"Fix bv triage guidance for current CLI and tombstone filtering","description":"Problem: AGENTS.md instructs agents to run bv --robot-triage/--robot-next, but installed bv does not provide those flags. Current fallback robot outputs can also return tombstoned beads, causing agents to pick deleted/merged work.

Scope:
- Update AGENTS.md bv section to include the current supported robot commands available in this environment.
- Add explicit fallback workflow using br when bv triage flags are unavailable.
- Add explicit guidance to treat tombstone/deleted beads as non-actionable and verify with br show/br ready before claiming.

Acceptance:
- AGENTS.md command examples align with installed bv --help output.
- Guidance prevents claiming tombstoned beads.
- Workflow remains non-interactive and agent-safe.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:44:16.730370772Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-26T06:45:35.502313199Z","closed_at":"2026-02-26T06:45:35.502286840Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-18j1","title":"E2E: OpenAI provider — basic message, streaming, tool use","description":"Create real E2E integration tests for the OpenAI provider (Responses API) using a live API key. Tests: (1) basic_message: send 'Say hello' to gpt-4o-mini and verify text response. (2) streaming: verify SSE events arrive correctly and accumulate to a complete response. (3) tool_use: trigger function calling and verify tool call structure. (4) model_selection: test with gpt-4o and gpt-4o-mini to verify both work. All tests log timing, token counts, response sizes. Skip if OPENAI_API_KEY not set.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T17:11:24.366952434Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T18:15:49.151340319Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T18:15:49.151305053Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-18j1","depends_on_id":"bd-1vfi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T17:17:19.148978314Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-18j1","depends_on_id":"bd-ovmd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T17:13:35.391313562Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":545,"issue_id":"bd-18j1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Acceptance criteria: run against configured OpenAI model(s) discovered at runtime; verify streaming SSE assembly + optional tool call shape; enforce short prompt/cost cap and emit JSONL telemetry with redacted request metadata.","created_at":"2026-02-06T17:29:25Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3dr9","title":"Editor: Tab path completion behavior","description":"# Goal
Implement Tab-based path completion in the editor (legacy: “Tab to complete paths”).

# Required Behavior
- When cursor is inside a path token (relative or absolute), pressing Tab should:
  - complete if there is a single unambiguous completion
  - otherwise open the autocomplete list filtered to path candidates

# Notes
- Use `fd` if available for better discovery and ignore rules.
- Respect `.gitignore` and avoid `target/` by default.

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Tab completes common relative paths quickly.
- [ ] Multi-candidate completions are presented via autocomplete UI.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss

 {"id":"bd-3dxz","title":"Memory profiling and 1-hour stress test (10+ concurrent extensions)","description":"# Memory profiling and 1-hour stress test (10+ concurrent extensions)

## What
Load 10+ extensions simultaneously, continuously fire events for 1 hour, verify:
- No memory leaks (RSS growth < 10% over the hour)
- No deadlocks
- No panics
- Consistent event dispatch latency (no degradation over time)

## Method
1. Load all 60 official extensions
2. Fire events in a loop at 100 events/second
3. Monitor RSS every 10 seconds
4. Monitor event dispatch latency continuously
5. After 1 hour, verify all metrics within bounds

## Target
- RSS growth < 10% over 1 hour
- No crashes/panics
- P99 latency does not increase by more than 2x over the hour
- All extensions still responsive at end of test

## Output
- Time series: RSS, latency P50/P99, event count
- Pass/fail determination","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:25:22.818613248Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T00:38:32.691877934Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T00:38:32.691694451Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3dxz","depends_on_id":"bd-139x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:25:22.824992692Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

This task is verification + closure, not greenfield implementation.","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:41:28Z"},{"id":1594,"issue_id":"bd-3eb4d","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implementation note: JSON mode ALREADY EXISTS and works:

 {"id":"bd-3ev","title":"Benchmark suite: startup/memory/streaming budgets","description":"Background:
- Performance targets: startup <100ms, binary <20MB, idle memory <50MB, smooth streaming (README + AGENTS).
- BENCHMARKS.md covers truncation/SSE baseline but not end-to-end budgets.

Scope / Steps:
1) Define benchmark harness for startup time (cold/warm), idle RSS, SSE throughput, tool-call latency.
2) Capture baseline numbers and encode budgets in BENCHMARKS.md + CI checks.
3) Add a legacy baseline comparison where feasible (pi-mono CLI timing).
4) Document measurement methodology + variance handling.

Acceptance:
- Benchmarks run locally and in CI with stable outputs.
- Budget regressions fail CI with clear diagnostics.
- BENCHMARKS.md includes methodology + latest baseline.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss

 {"id":"bd-qpm","title":"Apply theme colors to TUI components","description":"# Apply theme colors to TUI components

## Goal
Wire theme colors into lipgloss styles and glamour markdown rendering.

## Implementation

### Create Themed Styles
```rust
// src/interactive.rs

fn create_styles(theme: &Theme) -> Styles {
    Styles {
        message_user: lipgloss::Style::new()
            .foreground(lipgloss::Color::hex(&theme.colors.accent)),
        
        message_assistant: lipgloss::Style::new()
            .foreground(lipgloss::Color::hex(&theme.colors.foreground)),
        
        thinking: lipgloss::Style::new()
            .foreground(lipgloss::Color::hex(&theme.colors.muted))
            .italic(true),
        
        error: lipgloss::Style::new()
            .foreground(lipgloss::Color::hex(&theme.colors.error))
            .bold(true),
        
        success: lipgloss::Style::new()
            .foreground(lipgloss::Color::hex(&theme.colors.success)),
        
        border: lipgloss::Style::new()
            .border_foreground(lipgloss::Color::hex(&theme.ui.border)),
        
        // ... more styles
    }
}
```

### Apply to PiApp
```rust
impl PiApp {
    pub fn new(config: Config) -> Self {
        let theme = config.theme()
            .map(|name| Theme::load_by_name(&name).ok())
            .flatten()
            .unwrap_or_else(Theme::dark);
        
        let styles = create_styles(&theme);
        
        Self {
            theme,
            styles,
            // ...
        }
    }
}
```

### Apply to Glamour (Markdown)
```rust
fn create_glamour_style(theme: &Theme) -> glamour::Style {
    glamour::Style {
        document: glamour::StyleBlock {
            color: Some(theme.colors.foreground.clone()),
            background_color: Some(theme.colors.background.clone()),
            ..Default::default()
        },
        heading: glamour::StyleBlock {
            color: Some(theme.colors.accent.clone()),
            bold: Some(true),
            ..Default::default()
        },
        code: glamour::StyleBlock {
            color: Some(theme.syntax.string.clone()),
            ..Default::default()
        },
        // ... more elements
    }
}
```

 {"id":"bd-rhl","title":"Remove tokio and reqwest dependencies","description":"# Remove tokio and reqwest dependencies

## Goal
Complete the HTTP migration by removing tokio and reqwest from Cargo.toml.

## Background
After all providers migrate to asupersync, these dependencies become unused:
- tokio (async runtime)
- tokio-stream (stream utilities)
- reqwest (HTTP client)

This reduces binary size and simplifies the dependency tree.

## Implementation Steps

1. **Update Cargo.toml**
```toml
# Remove these lines:
tokio = { version = "1", features = ["full"] }
tokio-stream = "0.1"
reqwest = { version = "0.13", ... }

# Ensure asupersync is enabled (currently commented out):
asupersync = { path = "../asupersync", features = ["tls", "tls-native-roots", "sqlite"] }
```

2. **Update main.rs**
```rust
// Before
#[tokio::main]
async fn main() { ... }

// After - use asupersync runtime
fn main() {
    asupersync::runtime::Runtime::new()
        .block_on(async_main())
}
```

3. **Update all async code**
- Replace tokio::spawn with asupersync::spawn
- Replace tokio::time::sleep with asupersync equivalents
- Replace tokio::select! with asupersync patterns
- Update channel usage (if tokio channels used)

4. **Update test harness**
```rust
// Before
#[tokio::test]
async fn test_foo() { ... }

// After
#[asupersync::test]
async fn test_foo() { ... }
```

## Files Affected
- Cargo.toml
- src/main.rs
- src/agent.rs (spawn, select)
- src/tools.rs (bash timeout)
- All test files

## Dependencies
- bd-37l (Anthropic migrated)
- bd-1vo (OpenAI migrated)
- bd-1iy (Gemini migrated)
- bd-ivj (Azure migrated)

## Testing
- cargo build must succeed without tokio
- All tests must pass
- Binary size should decrease (~500KB-1MB)

## Risks
- Some tokio-specific features may need alternatives
- Test harness changes may be extensive

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tokio not in Cargo.toml
- [ ] reqwest not in Cargo.toml
- [ ] cargo build succeeds
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] Binary size reduced","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:33:32.853980666Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:24:53.392590688Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T08:37:48.931668013Z","close_reason":"Completed: tokio/reqwest fully removed; tests+clippy green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-rhl","depends_on_id":"bd-1iy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-rhl","depends_on_id":"bd-1vo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-rhl","depends_on_id":"bd-37l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-rhl","depends_on_id":"bd-gi3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-rhl","depends_on_id":"bd-ivj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}

- Clear normalization rules and tie‑breakers.
- Documented rationale for each weight.

This rubric must be simple enough to apply manually yet defensible.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:24:16.187391961Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T21:27:18.858409964Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T21:27:18.858321008Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-rhyl","depends_on_id":"bd-3o8d","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:24:16.202684254Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-rhyl","depends_on_id":"bd-97qh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T08:07:36.901909784Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":331,"issue_id":"bd-rhyl","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Background: Popularity alone is not enough; we need a balanced evaluation across usability and maintainability.

Reasoning: A weighted rubric makes selection reproducible and defensible.

Considerations: Keep the rubric simple, transparent, and aligned with Pi runtime constraints.","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:49:37Z"},{"id":381,"issue_id":"bd-rhyl","author":"LavenderRobin","text":"MAKE THIS EXECUTABLE + TESTED

To avoid “hand-wavy” selection, implement the rubric as a deterministic scoring function and test it.

Logging
- Emit a run summary (histograms + top-N lists + any manual overrides flagged).
","created_at":"2026-02-05T08:11:51Z"},{"id":587,"issue_id":"bd-rhyl","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Scoring rubric fully defined and implemented: EXTENSION_POPULARITY_CRITERIA.md has weighted criteria (Popularity 30, Adoption 15, Coverage 20, Activity 15, Compatibility 20) with normalization rules, tie-breakers (Coverage > Popularity > Recency > ID), and risk penalty (0-15). extension_scoring.rs implements all scoring functions deterministically. ext_score_candidates binary produces ranked JSON output. 60+ unit tests verify all thresholds, tie-breakers, and edge cases. 3 worked examples in docs.","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:27:03Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-s1hx","title":"Task: Implement input event (transforming, blocking)","description":"# Task: Implement Input Event (Transforming, Blocking)

## Objective

Dispatch the input event before processing user messages, allowing extensions to transform or block input.

## TypeScript Reference

```typescript
if (runner.hasHandlers('input')) {
    const inputResult = await runner.emit({
        type: 'input',
        content: userMessage.content,
        attachments: userMessage.attachments,
    });
    
    if (inputResult?.block) {
        console.log(inputResult.reason || 'Input blocked');
        return;
    }
    
    if (inputResult?.content) {
        userMessage.content = inputResult.content;
    }
}
```

## Event Details

### input Event
- **When**: Before processing user message
- **Purpose**: Transform, filter, or block input
- **Data**: content, attachments

### Return Value
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, Deserialize)]
pub struct InputEventResult {
    pub content: Option<String>,      // Transformed content
    pub block: bool,                   // If true, block processing
    pub reason: Option<String>,        // Reason for blocking
}
```

## Implementation

```rust
impl Agent {
    async fn process_user_input(
        &mut self,
        content: String,
        attachments: Vec<Attachment>,
    ) -> Result<Option<UserMessage>> {
        if let Some(dispatcher) = &self.event_dispatcher {
            if dispatcher.has_handlers("input") {
                let event = ExtensionEvent::Input {
                    content: content.clone(),
                    attachments: attachments.clone(),
                };
                
                if let Some(result) = dispatcher.dispatch::<InputEventResult>(event).await? {
                    if result.block {
                        let reason = result.reason.unwrap_or_else(|| "Input blocked".to_string());
                        self.display_message(&reason)?;
                        return Ok(None);
                    }
                    
                    let final_content = result.content.unwrap_or(content);
                    return Ok(Some(UserMessage { content: final_content, attachments }));
                }
            }
        }
        
        Ok(Some(UserMessage { content, attachments }))
    }
}
```

## Use Cases

1. **Macro Expansion**: Replace !date with current date
2. **Content Filter**: Block PII
3. **Translation**: Auto-translate input
4. **Logging**: Record all user inputs

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (5 cases)
1. test_no_handlers_uses_original
2. test_handler_returns_null_uses_original
3. test_handler_transforms_content
4. test_handler_blocks_input
5. test_block_reason_displayed

### E2E Test Script
Extension with macro expansion and content filter, JSONL logging.

## Dependencies

- Depends on: bd-jt3k (Wire Hostcall Dispatcher)
- Part of: bd-10vp (Extension Event Hook Dispatch)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] input event dispatched before processing
- [ ] Transformation applied correctly
- [ ] Blocking works with reason display
- [ ] 5 unit tests pass
- [ ] E2E test passes","notes":"Progress 2026-02-05: Implemented input event transform/block handling in interactive + AgentSession (apply_input_event_response helper, UpdateLastUserMessage for UI), removed duplicate input dispatch, and ensured before_agent_start dispatch still runs. Fixed clippy/test warnings in tests/provider_factory.rs, tests/agent_loop_vcr.rs, tests/e2e_cli.rs, tests/compaction.rs. Gates: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings. cargo test: 1 failure (e2e_interactive_smoke_tmux timeout)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:58:38.527571457Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T03:27:40.087499583Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T03:27:40.087436135Z","close_reason":"Implemented input event (transform/block) dispatch in interactive + AgentSession; tests/gates green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-s1hx","depends_on_id":"bd-10vp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:58:38.534290007Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-s1hx","depends_on_id":"bd-jt3k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:58:59.201969470Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-s1hx","depends_on_id":"bd-tg4w","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:58:58.239110985Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

@@ -441,64 +441,65 @@ bv is a graph-aware triage engine for Beads projects (`.beads/beads.jsonl`). It
 
 **Scope boundary:** bv handles *what to work on* (triage, priority, planning). For agent-to-agent coordination (messaging, work claiming, file reservations), use MCP Agent Mail.
 
-**CRITICAL: Use ONLY `--robot-*` flags. Bare `bv` launches an interactive TUI that blocks your session.**
+**CRITICAL: Use non-interactive flags (`--robot-*`, `--recipe`, `--as-of`, `--diff-since`, `--export-md`) only. Bare `bv` launches an interactive TUI that blocks your session.**
 
 ### The Workflow: Start With Triage
 
-**`bv --robot-triage` is your single entry point.** It returns:
-- `quick_ref`: at-a-glance counts + top 3 picks
-- `recommendations`: ranked actionable items with scores, reasons, unblock info
-- `quick_wins`: low-effort high-impact items
-- `blockers_to_clear`: items that unblock the most downstream work
-- `project_health`: status/type/priority distributions, graph metrics
-- `commands`: copy-paste shell commands for next steps
+Use this order of operations:
 
 ```bash
-bv --robot-triage        # THE MEGA-COMMAND: start here
-bv --robot-next          # Minimal: just the single top pick + claim command
+bv --robot-plan          # Primary triage surface (tracks + highest-impact summary)
+bv --robot-priority      # Priority sanity check and suggested re-ranking
+bv --robot-insights      # Deep graph metrics when needed
+br ready --json          # Ground-truth actionable issues from Beads
 ```
 
+If your local `bv` build supports `--robot-triage`, you can still use it. If not, `--robot-plan` + `br ready --json` is the required fallback.
+
+**CRITICAL Tombstone Guard:** `bv` output can include `status = tombstone` items in some versions. Tombstones are deleted/merged issues and are **never actionable**.
+
+Before claiming work from `bv`, always verify status with `br`:
+
+```bash
+br show <issue-id> --json | jq -r '.[0].status'
+# Only proceed if status is open/in_progress and the issue is not deleted/tombstoned.
+```
+
+If `br ready --json` is empty and `bv` only surfaces tombstones, do not claim tombstoned items. Create or refine a real bead and proceed.
+
 ### Command Reference
 
 **Planning:**
 | Command | Returns |
 |---------|---------|
 | `--robot-plan` | Parallel execution tracks with `unblocks` lists |
 | `--robot-priority` | Priority misalignment detection with confidence |
+| `--robot-recipes` | Available recipe filters for scoped triage |
 
 **Graph Analysis:**
 | Command | Returns |
 |---------|---------|
 | `--robot-insights` | Full metrics: PageRank, betweenness, HITS, eigenvector, critical path, cycles, k-core, articulation points, slack |
-| `--robot-label-health` | Per-label health: `health_level`, `velocity_score`, `staleness`, `blocked_count` |
-| `--robot-label-flow` | Cross-label dependency: `flow_matrix`, `dependencies`, `bottleneck_labels` |
-| `--robot-label-attention [--attention-limit=N]` | Attention-ranked labels |
 
 **History & Change Tracking:**
 | Command | Returns |
 |---------|---------|
-| `--robot-history` | Bead-to-commit correlations |
 | `--robot-diff --diff-since <ref>` | Changes since ref: new/closed/modified issues, cycles |
 
 **Other:**
 | Command | Returns |
 |---------|---------|
-| `--robot-burndown <sprint>` | Sprint burndown, scope changes, at-risk items |
-| `--robot-forecast <id\|all>` | ETA predictions with dependency-aware scheduling |
-| `--robot-alerts` | Stale issues, blocking cascades, priority mismatches |
-| `--robot-suggest` | Hygiene: duplicates, missing deps, label suggestions |
-| `--robot-graph [--graph-format=json\|dot\|mermaid]` | Dependency graph export |
-| `--export-graph <file.html>` | Interactive HTML visualization |
+| `--recipe <name>` | Apply recipe filters (for example `actionable`, `high-impact`) |
+| `--export-md <file.md>` | Markdown status/export report |
 
 ### Scoping & Filtering
 
 ```bash
-bv --robot-plan --label backend              # Scope to label's subgraph
-bv --robot-insights --as-of HEAD~30          # Historical point-in-time
+bv --robot-plan --as-of HEAD~30              # Historical point-in-time
 bv --recipe actionable --robot-plan          # Pre-filter: ready to work
-bv --recipe high-impact --robot-triage       # Pre-filter: top PageRank
-bv --robot-triage --robot-triage-by-track    # Group by parallel work streams
-bv --robot-triage --robot-triage-by-label    # Group by domain
+bv --recipe high-impact --robot-plan         # Pre-filter: top-impact set
+bv --robot-priority                          # Cross-check priority drift
+bv --robot-recipes                           # Discover installed recipe names
 ```
 
 ### Understanding Robot Output
@@ -515,9 +516,9 @@ bv --robot-triage --robot-triage-by-label    # Group by domain
 ### jq Quick Reference
 
 ```bash
-bv --robot-triage | jq '.quick_ref'                        # At-a-glance summary
-bv --robot-triage | jq '.recommendations[0]'               # Top recommendation
 bv --robot-plan | jq '.plan.summary.highest_impact'        # Best unblock target
+bv --robot-plan | jq '.plan.tracks[0].items[0]'            # First candidate in first track
+bv --robot-priority | jq '.recommendations[0]'             # Top priority recommendation
 bv --robot-insights | jq '.status'                         # Check metric readiness
 bv --robot-insights | jq '.Cycles'                         # Circular deps (must fix!)
 ```
